problem_statement:
Given an expression, A, with operands and operators (OR, AND, XOR), in how many
    ways can you evaluate the expression to be true, by grouping it in different ways?.

examples.explanation:
The only way to get the result as true is:
(T) | (T&F) = T|F = T 

Example 2:

There are 2 possible ways to get the result as true:
		i. (F|T) ^ F = T ^ F = T
		ii. F | (T^F) = F | T = T
